metadata.archetype_justification: Zipkin has persistent data stores (Cassandra, Elasticsearch, MySQL), exposes both read and write endpoints, and manages trace data entity lifecycle.

findings.title: No Machine Identity Authentication
findings.description: The Zipkin server has no authentication mechanism. No OAuth2, API key, mTLS, or any identity-based access control is implemented. The application is designed to be accessed without authentication, relying entirely on network-level security.
findings.gap: No machine identity authentication exists. An agent calling this system cannot be identified, attributed, or distinguished from any other caller.
findings.recommendation: Deploy an API Gateway or reverse proxy in front of Zipkin that enforces API key or OAuth2 client credentials authentication and passes caller identity headers.

blocker_remediation.immediate: Deploy an API Gateway with OAuth2 client credentials or API key authentication in front of Zipkin.
blocker_remediation.target_state: Every API request is authenticated with a machine identity principal. The authenticated principal is logged in access logs for attribution.

findings.title: No Scoped Permissions
findings.description: No authorization model exists within Zipkin. All API endpoints are accessible to any caller without permission checks. There are no IAM policies, role definitions, or scope-based access controls.
findings.gap: Without scoped permissions, an agent identity cannot be restricted to read-only access on specific resources.
findings.recommendation: Implement API Gateway resource policies that separate read (GET) and write (POST) operations with different authorization levels.

compensating_controls: Deploy API Gateway with method-level authorization (allow GET, deny POST for read-only agents)
Use network segmentation to expose only query endpoints to agent callers

findings.title: No Action-Level Authorization
findings.description: No action-level authorization exists. The server does not distinguish between read and write operations from an access control perspective.
findings.gap: Cannot enforce action-level policies such as 'agent can read traces but cannot submit spans' at the application layer.
findings.recommendation: Implement authorization at the platform layer (API Gateway or service mesh) with per-method policies.

compensating_controls: API Gateway method-level policies (GET vs POST)
Service mesh authorization policies (Istio AuthorizationPolicy)

findings.title: No Immutable Audit Logging
findings.description: No audit logging configuration exists within the application. No CloudTrail, no immutable log storage, and no per-request principal logging. Without authentication (AUTH-Q1), audit logging of principals is structurally impossible.
findings.gap: No audit trail identifies which caller performed which operation.
findings.recommendation: After implementing authentication (AUTH-Q1), add per-request audit logging that captures the authenticated principal, action, and timestamp in an immutable store.

ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents do not execute write operations, so audit logging is important but not a deployment blocker.
compensating_controls: Enable access logging at the API Gateway layer to capture request metadata
Deploy CloudTrail for API Gateway access logging

findings.title: No Agent Identity Suspension Mechanism
findings.description: No identity management exists within Zipkin. There is no mechanism to suspend, revoke, or disable any caller's access because there is no identity system.
findings.gap: Cannot isolate a misbehaving agent without network-level blocking.
findings.recommendation: When API Gateway authentication is added (AUTH-Q1), ensure API keys can be individually revoked without affecting other agent identities.

compensating_controls: API Gateway API key revocation
Network-level IP blocking via security groups or WAF rules

findings.title: No API-Layer Rate Limiting for Query Endpoints
findings.description: Storage-level throttling exists via ThrottledStorageComponent using Netflix concurrency-limits for adaptive write-path rate limiting. However, no rate limiting exists on the query API layer.
findings.gap: Query endpoints are unprotected from agent traffic storms. A runaway agent loop would not be throttled.
findings.recommendation: Add API Gateway throttling configuration or WAF rate rules protecting query endpoints from excessive agent traffic.

compensating_controls: Deploy AWS API Gateway or WAF with rate limiting rules on query endpoints
Configure Armeria server-level request rate limiting

findings.title: No PII Redaction in Logs
findings.description: No PII scrubbing or redaction middleware in logging configuration. User-defined span tags could contain PII if instrumented applications pass it.
findings.gap: No log redaction middleware for PII that may arrive via span annotations.
findings.recommendation: Implement tag-level filtering on span ingestion to drop or mask known PII patterns. Add log redaction middleware.

compensating_controls: Enforce instrumentation guidelines upstream to prevent PII in span tags
Deploy log filtering at the centralized logging layer

findings.title: No Machine-Readable API Specification
findings.description: No OpenAPI, Swagger, AsyncAPI, or machine-readable API specification exists. API defined only in Armeria Java annotations.
findings.gap: Agent tool definitions must be manually authored and will drift from implementation.
findings.recommendation: Generate OpenAPI 3.0 specification from existing API routes in ZipkinQueryApiV2.java and ZipkinHttpCollector.java.

compensating_controls: Generate OpenAPI spec from Armeria annotations
Manually maintain an OpenAPI spec as a documentation artifact

findings.title: No Structured Error Responses
findings.description: Error responses use plain text bodies with HTTP status codes. No structured error format with error codes or retryable indicators.
findings.gap: Agents cannot programmatically distinguish error types or determine retryability.
findings.recommendation: Implement structured JSON error response format with error_code, message, and retryable fields.

compensating_controls: Agent tool definitions can map HTTP status codes to retry behavior
Wrap Zipkin API in an intermediary that adds structured error envelopes

findings.title: Non-Structured Logging and Limited Request Correlation
findings.description: Self-tracing available via Brave. Logging includes traceId/spanId in pattern. Logs are not structured JSON. No inbound request correlation IDs.
findings.gap: Non-JSON logs, no request correlation IDs for inbound API calls.
findings.recommendation: Enable structured JSON logging and ensure inbound API requests generate correlation IDs.

compensating_controls: Enable self-tracing (SELF_TRACING_ENABLED=true)
Deploy structured logging sidecar

findings.title: No Alerting Configuration
findings.description: Prometheus metrics exposed via Micrometer. No alerting rules, CloudWatch alarms, or alerting configuration defined.
findings.gap: No alerting thresholds for agent-consumed APIs.
findings.recommendation: Define alerting rules for query API latency p99 and error rate thresholds.

compensating_controls: Configure Prometheus alerting rules externally
Deploy CloudWatch alarms if running on AWS

findings.title: No Breaking Change Detection in CI
findings.description: API uses versioned URL paths (/api/v2/). No formal schema versioning, no breaking change detection in CI, no consumer-driven contract tests.
findings.gap: No breaking change detection mechanism in CI pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenAPI spec diffing to CI pipeline to detect breaking changes.

compensating_controls: Pin agent tool definitions to /api/v2/ version path
Monitor API response schema changes via integration tests

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No IaC files exist in this repository. No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm, or Kustomize configurations.
findings.gap: Agent-facing infrastructure not governed as code in this repo.
findings.recommendation: Create IaC definitions for the Zipkin deployment surface including API gateway, networking, and access controls.

compensating_controls: IaC may be maintained in a separate infrastructure repository
Docker Compose files provide deployment topology documentation

findings.title: No API Contract Testing in CI
findings.description: Comprehensive CI/CD with 8 GitHub Actions workflows, multi-JDK testing, Docker integration tests, Trivy security scanning. No API contract testing or breaking change detection.
findings.gap: CI pipeline tests functional correctness but does not validate API contracts.
findings.recommendation: Add API contract testing to CI pipeline — consumer-driven contract tests or OpenAPI spec validation.

compensating_controls: Integration tests validate API behavior indirectly
Multi-JDK testing ensures compatibility

findings.title: No Automated Rollback Mechanism
findings.description: Docker image versioning enables rollback by pulling previous versions. No explicit automated rollback mechanism (blue/green, canary, CodeDeploy) defined.
findings.gap: Rollback relies on external deployment tooling.
findings.recommendation: Define deployment strategy with automated rollback triggers in the deployment environment.

compensating_controls: Docker image versioning enables manual rollback
External orchestration (Kubernetes, ECS) may provide automated rollback
